title: Git Recovery & History Rewriting
description: Learn to use reflog, reset, revert, restore, and fsck to recover lost commits and undo changes safely.

tasks.prompt: View the reference log for HEAD movements.
tasks.explanation: reflog records where HEAD and branches have been recently.

tasks.prompt: Soft reset to the previous commit, keeping changes staged.
tasks.explanation: --soft moves HEAD but leaves the index and working tree unchanged.

tasks.prompt: Mixed reset to HEAD~1, unstaging changes but keeping files modified.
tasks.hint: Use git reset HEAD~1 (default mixed mode).
tasks.explanation: Mixed reset moves HEAD and unstages changes without deleting file edits.

tasks.prompt: Hard reset the current branch to commit 'a1b2c3d', discarding local changes.
tasks.explanation: --hard resets HEAD, index, and working tree to the target commit.

tasks.prompt: Create a new commit that reverts the changes introduced by commit 'a1b2c3d'.
tasks.hint: Use git revert with the commit hash.
tasks.explanation: git revert safely undoes a commit by adding an inverse commit.

tasks.prompt: Discard uncommitted changes in 'app.py' and restore the last committed version.
tasks.explanation: git restore resets a working tree file to the index or HEAD version.
git restore --staged removes a file from the index without touching the working tree.

tasks.prompt: Recover the branch state from reflog entry HEAD@{2}.
tasks.explanation: Checking out a reflog entry lets you inspect or recover prior states.

tasks.prompt: Find dangling commits and blobs using git fsck.
tasks.explanation: git fsck --lost-found identifies unreachable objects that may be recoverable.

tasks.prompt: Revert the merge commit 'm3rge01' with parent 1 specified (mainline).
tasks.explanation: -m 1 tells git which parent to treat as mainline when reverting a merge.
